mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, the Federal Reserve maintains a restrictive monetary stance that keeps real rates elevated. This suppresses equity valuations and increases demand for safe-haven Treasury bonds, driving 10-year yields downward over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into fixed income.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This demand destruction triggers flight-to-safety behavior, compressing 10-year Treasury yields downward as investors anticipate slower economic growth and potential Fed rate cuts within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.25 percentage points in prior month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in fewer than 50% of instances following UNRATE increases > 0.25pp, or correlation between UNRATE changes and yield direction is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously, aggregate demand weakens, reducing inflation expectations and long-term growth forecasts. This dual contraction drives 10-year Treasury yields lower as the market reprices terminal rate expectations downward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% and HOUST declines > 5% in prior month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of dual-contraction instances, or the interaction effect (joint decline) produces no statistically significant incremental predictive power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 102.5 indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization. Sustained high production signals inflationary pressure and justifies higher real rates. This supply strength pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days as the market prices in persistent inflation and Fed hold-steady expectations.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO > 102.5 with positive growth, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly determine real rate expectations. When CPIAUCSL growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains static or declines, real rates compress, reducing the attractiveness of long-duration Treasuries. This real rate squeeze drives 10-year yields upward over the next 5 trading days as investors demand compensation for eroding purchasing power.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.4% and FEDFUNDS unchanged or declining
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or inflation surprise regime
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 50% of real-rate-squeeze instances, or the interaction term (inflation acceleration with static rates) adds no incremental explanatory power beyond individual components (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth above 150,000 persons per month signals sustained labor demand and wage pressure. Strong employment growth validates Fed rate maintenance and increases long-term inflation expectations, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal rate and inflation risk premium.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 150,000 persons for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: tight labor market with sustained job creation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 55% of instances following sustained PAYEMS growth > 150k, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window during tight labor market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth signals underlying economic momentum. When GDPC1 accelerates above trend (annualized growth > 2.5%), the market reprices long-term growth expectations upward, increasing terminal rate assumptions and pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 60 trading days. This growth-driven yield rise reflects stronger future cash flows and reduced recession risk.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate > 2.5% in latest quarter
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 50% of above-trend growth quarters, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) together capture consumer confidence and discretionary spending. When HOUST declines below 1,200 thousand units while RSAFS remains flat or declining, this signals demand-side weakness that triggers expectations of Fed rate cuts within 60 trading days. The combination drives 10-year Treasury yields downward as investors price in lower terminal rates and reduced long-term growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,200 thousand units and RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.5%
regime_hint: demand-side weakness with housing market softening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of weak-demand instances, or the joint signal (low housing + weak retail) produces no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (Vuong test p > 0.20) over 3-year rolling windows.
